title: Apply for a vanity plate
description: Apply for a personalised vanity number plate for your vehicle.
summary: A vanity plate is a personalised number plate. Instead of a regular number, you choose the letters and numbers you want – for example, your initials or a meaningful word.

An Advisory Committee at the Barbados Licensing Authority reviews every application. They make sure the plate is not offensive, contentious, sensitive, sexual or in breach of a trademark. They reserve the right to accept or reject any request.
The first-time registration is $3,000 BBD. After that, you pay $1,000 BBD each year to keep the plate. These fees are clearly stated on the form itself.
If the plate you want is already in use, or the Committee rejects it, you can amend your request and apply again.

items: your full name, address, phone and email
your current registration number, if you have one
the wording or numbers you want on the vanity plate

items.name: Proof of payment for the registration fee

how_long: The Advisory Committee usually decides within 4 to 6 weeks.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Application for an Approved Vanity Plate form to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

edge_cases.heading: If your application is rejected
content: The Advisory Committee will give a reason. You can amend your request and apply again.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: ‘The Advisory Committee usually decides within 4 to 6 weeks’ is an estimate. The form does not state a processing time.
‘Proof of payment of the registration fee’ requirement is inferred. The form does not list documents to attach.
